name: S&P 500
summary: Day 80 of Hormuz blockade (Monday). **MONDAY MAY 18 MAXIMAL-PRESSURE TAPE — TUESDAY MAY 19 NSC MEETING IS OPERATIONAL BINARY.** Friday closes carried into Monday: S&P 7,408.50 (-1.24%), NASDAQ 26,225.14 (-1.54%), Dow 49,526.17 (-1.07%). **Monday Asian/European/global tape**: Brent gapped to $111.47 (+2.0%, StanChart $130-145 path activates — 2-session sustain met); USD/INR ₹96.29 record low (Day 6 of 5-7 trigger fires); Sensex/Nifty open -1.30%/-1.04%; KOSPI +0.6% (rally contradicting prep tape — Friday's -6.1% was the panic); MSCI EM -3.4% (composite EM rout — India structural + Brazil idiosyncratic + Korea Samsung). **Sector cascade**: Energy XLE +2.4% (+5.1% 1M, +11.3% 3M); Exxon +4.1%; **Defense ITA -3.2% (NOT bidding on prep news = structural de-rating)** — Lockheed -0.8% / -15.1% 1M, cleanest 'this is a maturing crisis, not a fresh war' signal; SMH -3.8% (Samsung+rates, ~75% non-crisis); Uranium URA -4.9% (positioning unwind on Iran HEU concession narrative); Shipping BOAT -2.2%; CF Industries +2.3% (fertilizer trade); MSCI EM EEM -3.4%. **VIX 18.43 still sub-20** — vol complex has NOT capitulated despite weekend kinetic tape. **Tuesday NSC operational binary**: strike order → S&P -3-5% snap, Brent +$8-15, VIX 24-28; back-off → S&P +1-2%, Brent retraces $5-8, VIX 14-16; nothing operational → Branch 2 holds. **April PPI +1.4% + Warsh effective May 16 + Trump-Netanyahu coordination + Saudi/UAE drone events = stagflation regime layered with kinetic-prep tail-risk.** Crisis vs non-crisis split for S&P -1.2% Friday: ~0.5% crisis (Trump reversal + Iran ultimatum); ~0.5% rates/Fed (PPI + Warsh); ~0.2% sympathy with KOSPI/EM. **PRIOR (May 17): US/INDIA/EUROPE EQUITY MARKETS CLOSED SUNDAY — OPERATIVE PRICES ARE FRIDAY MAY 15 CLOSES. Monday May 18 Asian open is the first repricing event for accumulated weekend news (NYT preparation report + Trump 'Calm Before the Storm' Truth Social + Iran-Kharg air-defense reinforcement + Pakistan-Naqvi mediation visit).** Friday closes carried into Sunday: S&P 7,408.50 (-1.24%), NASDAQ 26,225.14 (-1.54%), Dow 49,526.17 (-1.07%, lost 50K), SMH $556.34 (-3.8%), VIX 18.43. **Monday May 18 binary tests**: (1) Brent close vs $108 — StanChart $130-145 trigger; (2) VIX print sub-20 vs gap to 23-24; (3) USD/INR ₹96 trigger (Day 5 of 5-7 window); (4) RBI Friday weekly reserves print (delayed from May 16; will land Monday-Tuesday); (5) KOSPI gap and Samsung Monday open; (6) Pakistan-Naqvi readout — operational mediation deliverable vs ceremonial diplomatic; (7) Defense ETF response to NYT report — snap-back (proves de-rating overshoot) vs continued weakness (de-rating structural); (8) VLCC TD3C Week 19 release; (9) TTF Gas direction — €50.85 sustained vs retrace on no-LNG-incident weekend. **Three-force stagflation regime carries from Friday into Monday** (PPI shock + Warsh + Trump-Iran reversal); crisis component ~30% (NYT preparation tail-risk adds modestly); rates component ~70% (structural). **PRIOR (May 16):** PAPER-EQUITY LEG OF CYCLE-EXTREME BIFURCATION RESOLVED VIOLENTLY ON MAY 15 — S&P -1.24% to 7,408.50 (off Thursday's 7,501.24 record); NASDAQ -1.54% to 26,225.14; Dow -1.07% lost 50K (-537.29 pts to 49,526.17); SMH -3.8% to $556.34; VIX 17.26→18.43 (+6.8%); 10Y Treasury +14bp to 4.595% (highest since Feb 2025), 30Y +11bp to 5.121% (highest since May 2025) — Bloomberg labelled it 'the worst weekly Treasury rout in a year.'** **THREE-FORCE STAGFLATION COLLISION**: (1) **April PPI +1.4% m/m / +6% YoY (BLS May 13)** — biggest single-month jump since December 2022, Core PPI +1% vs 0.4% est, ~60% of rise was in services, energy at the root; combined with April CPI 3.8% YoY (May 12) closes near-term Fed easing door indefinitely. (2) **Kevin Warsh confirmed by Senate 54-45 on May 13 replacing Powell effective today May 16** — Warsh wants to shrink balance sheet and rely on traditional rate tool; markets had been pricing dovish-Rieder hopium; 'investors now expecting Fed to keep rate unchanged for the rest of the year — or even raise rates if inflation worsens.' (3) **Trump immediately reverted to escalatory rhetoric post-summit** ('decimation' Truth Social + Hannity 'not patient' + Detroit News 'didn't ask for favours') and on **Saturday May 16 sent Iran a new nuclear ultimatum letter** demanding full dismantling + halt all enrichment + end proxy support within 2-month compliance. Composite mechanism: PPI lands → bond yields gap higher → DXY +0.4% to 99.27 → gold underperforms on real-yield headwind ($4,564 -1.83%) → equity multiples compress → Trump reversal layered on top → oil rips → energy stocks bid (XLE +2.4%, Exxon +4.1%), everything else sold. **Tech took the brunt**: Intel -6%, AMD -5.7%, Micron -6.6%, Nvidia -4.4%. **Crisis vs non-crisis split for S&P -1.2%**: ~0.5% crisis (Trump reversal + Iran ultimatum); ~0.5% rates/Fed (PPI absorption + Warsh transition); ~0.2% sympathy with KOSPI/EM. **Crisis component reverses if Iran responds within 2-month window; rates component does not.** **VIX 18.43 still sub-20 — vol complex has NOT capitulated**: (a) move was orderly across asset classes; (b) summer ranges typically compress; (c) NVDA earnings May 20 keeps vol-sellers active; (d) systematic dealers still long gamma above 7,400 SPX. **VIX >22 sustained 2 sessions = regime confirmation.** Other markets: KOSPI -6.1% (Samsung -8.6% on confirmed 18-day strike + Korea AI-tax + foreign net sell ₩1.8T 7th straight session); MSCI EM -3.4% (composite EM rout); DAX -2.1% (HeidelbergCement -7.16%); Nikkei -2.0%; FTSE -1.7%; Sensex/Nifty -0.2% (relatively immune — Modi-MBZ MoUs signed structural offset + RBI defense holding). Silver -10.6% one-day to $77.52 (Goldman: rising yields + dual identity); Gold -2.6% to $4,555.80; Copper -4.8%; Platinum -4.9%; Aluminum -2.6%; Uranium URA -4.9%; Defense ITA -3.2% (Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended 45 days + Lockheed Q1 drag); Frontline -1.1%; Lockheed -0.8% / -15.1% 1M. **The 'one side resolves violently within 5-10 sessions' framing from yesterday's brief is now Day 1 of the resolution: paper-equity capitulated FIRST, paper-Brent retraced modestly but did not deflate, physical-shipping rates stayed elevated. Resolution direction is toward crisis confirmation, NOT peace.** PRIOR (May 15): **B6/C9 RISK-ASSET COMPLACENCY AT CYCLE HIGH — S&P 500 7,501.24 (+0.8% 1D, +6.8% 1M, +9.6% 3M) ABOVE 7,500 FIRST TIME AT RECORD; NASDAQ Composite 26,635.22 (+0.9% 1D, +10.9% 1M, +18.0% 3M) RECORD CLOSE; Dow Jones 50,063.46 (+0.7% 1D, +3.3% 1M) RECLAIMED 50,000.** **VIX 17.26 (-3.4% 1D, -5.0% 1M, -14.9% 3M) = CYCLE LOW since war began Feb 28**. This complacency level is registering on a day with two new kinetic incidents (Hui Chuan + Haj Ali), an Israeli KIA from Litani-induced trigger window (Dagan), Israel-Lebanon Washington talks final 48-hour window before ceasefire expiry, Trump's 1-week clock window still open through May 20, Iranian Army spokesperson 'surprising options' rhetoric, Samsung 18-day strike scheduled to begin May 21, and SK Hynix capacity at 'essentially zero.' Brent rose to $106.89 (+1.11% — refusing to fall on summit), VLCC TD3C confirmed at $467,408/day via Baltic Exchange Week 17. **THIS IS THE CLEANEST PAPER-PHYSICAL BIFURCATION READING OF THE CYCLE**: paper assets pricing structural-stability + AI-cycle dominance, physical complex pricing intact-blockade + escalation-tail-risk. **ONE SIDE RESOLVES VIOLENTLY WITHIN 5-10 SESSIONS.** Sectoral repricing (markets dossier C3): **Aluminum -8.9% (4-year-high long blown out on Trump-Xi Hormuz unwind); Silver -7.0% / Gold -2.2% / Platinum -3.5% / Copper -3.1% (precious-industrial double-hit, tail-risk-removed trade); RBOB Gasoline -3.2% (refiner slate unwind, NOT demand collapse); CF Industries -2.0% (fertilizer-trade unwind despite Qatar urea still halted).** Lockheed Martin $520.41 (+0.1% 1D, -14.8% 1M, -19.5% 3M) = defense de-rating continues. **Nikkei -2.0% on BOJ 6-3 hawkish split keeping yen-carry unwind live**; Hang Seng -1.9% on Trump-Xi disappointment (Foreign Policy: 'failed to secure major concessions'); KOSPI -6.1% (single largest equity move of cycle on Samsung strike + Iran rhetoric + foreign profit-taking). Shanghai -1.4% on PBOC fix 6.8415 vs Reuters estimate 6.7976 (Beijing tolerance for further yuan depreciation). **Per markets dossier H reversibility**: if Trump-Xi joint statement produces no operational follow-through by May 20 and Brent breaks $108 sustained 2 sessions, VIX has 5-7 points of repricing upside (S&P -3-5% snap). MSCI EM ETF (EEM) $67.38 +0.3% 1D / +8.3% 1M / +10.7% 3M. US 10Y 4.46% (-0.4% 1D); 2Y 3.59% (-0.3% 1D); DXY 99.13 (+0.3% 1D). **April CPI 3.8% YoY + April PPI +1.4% m/m closed near-term Fed easing door**; Morgan Stanley moved rate-cut call to 2027. NVDA reports May 20 — positioning ahead of it supports tech bid. **C3 CRISIS-TRADE UNWIND CONTINUES — S&P 500 7,444.25 (+0.6% 1D, +6.8% 1M, +8.8% 3M); NASDAQ Composite 26,402.34 (+1.2% 1D, +11.7% 1M, +16.9% 3M); Dow 49,693.20 (-0.1% 1D, +2.4% 1M, +0.3% 3M).** VIX 17.87 (-0.7% 1D, -2.7% 1M, -13.3% 3M) = sub-18 on a 1-week-clock-day; markets pricing Trump-Xi pause. SMH $572.46 +2.0% 1D (+26.7% 1M, +40.5% 3M). **Sectoral repricing (markets dossier C3): Lockheed Martin $519.94 (-0.2% 1D, -15.0% 1M, -19.5% 3M) = DEFENSE DE-RATING IN MOTION** — Q1 EPS miss + F-16 program delays + negative FCF + falling Iran tensions; the -15% 1M is the **single largest move in the bellwether complex** and signals market pricing crisis maturation, not escalation. **Defense ETF ITA $225.71 (+0.1% 1D, -4.1% 1M, -5.2% 3M)** — confirms broader defense rotation. **CF Industries $125.50 (-3.8% 1D, +5.5% 1M)** = Modi May 10-11 fertilizer-cut-50% appeal demand-shock + Trump-Xi trade-not-war Gulf urea normalization (~80% crisis-trade-unwind). **Cheniere (LNG) $239.38 (-2.0% 1D, -7.6% 1M, +9.6% 3M)** = Q1 $3.5bn hedge loss + LNG infrastructure unwind on Hormuz-tail-risk diminishment (~85% crisis-trade-unwind). **Frontline (Tankers) $37.19 (-3.5% 1D, +8.9% 1M, +21.7% 3M)** = Evercore ISI downgrade citing 2027-28 orderbook oversupply despite VLCC TD3C ~$420K/day spike — paper-physical bifurcation within same company's price discovery; **Frontline equity SELLING vs Frontline tanker-day-rate SPIKING = leading indicator of de-escalation if equity stays sold; leading indicator of physical disruption if VLCC rates stay elevated.** Nutrien $71.29 (-1.0% 1D, -3.2% 1M); Linde $513.26 (+1.9% 1D, +2.7% 1M, +6.8% 3M) industrial-gas bid intact; BASF €54.00 (+1.5% 1D, +3.0% 1M); Exxon Mobil $151.57 (+0.6% 1D, +1.6% 1M, +3.7% 3M) major-oil NOT bidding even on Brent +56.7% 3M (XOM only +3.7% 3M); XLE $57.63 (+0.1% 1D, +3.0% 1M, +7.9% 3M); URA $53.31 (-1.9% 1D, +0.9% 1M, +3.9% 3M); BOAT $42.41 (-0.3% 1D, +3.6% 1M, +11.5% 3M); Aluminum -5.6% 1D (position unwind + Discovery Alert cycle-low rebound monetised + demand caution + USD bid); RBOB Gasoline -4.5% 1D (profit-taking after +80% 3M + surprise gasoline stocks build + SPR 8.6 MMb week drawdown easing supply panic + Trump-Xi summit). Agriculture rally: Wheat +2.7% 1D / +15.0% 1M (Black Sea + corrective buying); Corn +3.2% 1D (Black Sea + Trump-Xi tariff dialogue, China #2 corn importer); Rough Rice +2.6% 1D / +14.5% 1M (India export-restriction signal still live + momentum); Sugar +2.5% 1D / +10.8% 1M (Brazil cane crush slowdown + USD/BRL +1.5%); Palm Oil -1.3%. **MSCI EM ETF (EEM) $67.21 (+2.1% 1D, +8.0% 1M, +10.4% 3M)** = clean market vote on Trump-Xi 'partners not rivals' opening (~70% Trump-Xi / ~30% AI cycle). **Bovespa (Brazil) 177,098.00 (-1.8% 1D, -10.3% 1M, -5.0% 3M)** = idiosyncratic Bolsonaro-Banco Master scandal + April inflation accelerated + USD/BRL +1.5% (~80% domestic). US 10Y yield 4.48% (+0.4% 1D, +5.3% 1M, +10.5% 3M) — CPI absorption; Fed easing closed. **A2 1-WEEK CLOCK CLOSED TODAY (Day 7/7) WITHOUT STRIKE ORDER — forced-pause-via-Beijing validated.** Trump publicly opened HEU-via-China custodian door at Trump-Xi summit Day 1; Vance softer line; Pentagon Sledgehammer naming. Branch re-pricing: kinetic ~20-25% (DOWN); extended-economic-warfare ~52-57% (UP modestly — Sledgehammer + IRGC exercise); negotiated-resolution ~22-27% (UP — China-custodian door). **Per markets dossier H reversibility map: VIX 12-14 (-22 to -33%) if MOU signs by May 21; VIX 26-32 (+45 to +80%) if Hormuz remains closed through June; if Trump-Xi summit fails to deliver framework, VIX +30% spike to 23-24 by Monday is base case.** PRIOR (May 13): **A2 APRIL CPI 3.8% ABSORBED — S&P 500 -0.29% MAY 12 TO 7,392 (off May 11 record 7,412.84); NASDAQ -0.7% to 26,088.20; Dow +0.1% to 49,760.56; SMH -2.6% to $561.25 (profit-take after vertical AI run; TAIEX -1.3% sympathy); VIX FELL -2.1% TO 17.99 = THE PARADOX OF THE DAY.** Expected on normal escalation+CPI+Litani day: VIX +5-10% intraday. Got: -2.1%. **VIX paradox is the DOMINANT MARKET READ — markets pricing TRUMP-IN-BEIJING as forced kinetic pause through May 15-16; options-positioning desk reads 48-hour vol kill via timeline mechanics; EIA forecast cut to $94.85 (TASS) = institutional vote against Brent fear premium; Aramco/ADNOC quiet limited Hormuz resume (World Oil May 8) confirms physical supply ~90% preserved through East-West routing; Iran missile fire rate -92% (JPost + CNN intel) = capability-vs-willingness gap publicly quantified. Investor implication is ASYMMETRIC: if Trump returns from Beijing without an order, VIX 16-handle plausible + Brent retraces $97-100. If he returns AND orders, VIX +30% intraday + Brent $115-120. Summit is binary digital, not continuous variable.** US 10Y yield 4.46% (+1.2% 1D, +3.9% 1M, +8.7% 3M) — CPI absorbed AND upstream rate. **A2 CPI 3.8% = first cycle inflation print to fully reflect oil pass-through; energy contributed >40% of headline gain; gasoline +28.4% YoY; shelter +0.6%; BLS itself flagged 'a problem beyond Iran war impacts'** — closes door on near-term Fed easing; URA -5.0% (positioning unwind); gold +0.8% only (real-yield channel competing); Cu +2.5% / Ag +2.6% (China-reflation Trump-Xi summit bid). **Sectoral split:** XLE $57.57 (+0.7% 1D, +0.8% 1M, +6.6% 3M); ITA $225.39 (-0.3% 1D, -3.2% 1M, -4.0% 3M, defense falsified-trade pattern intact); SMH $561.25 (-2.6% 1D, +26.6% 1M, +37.7% 3M); BOAT $42.53 (-0.1%); URA $54.35 (-5.0%); LMT $521.00 (+1.7% 1D, -15.9% 1M); LNG $244.31 (+1.5%); FRO $38.53 (+1.0%); CF $130.39 (+4.7% Day 2 of fertilizer compounding); XOM $150.63 (+0.6%); NTR $71.98 (+1.0%); BASF €53.20 (-0.5%); LIN $503.87 (-0.1%). **C5 INDUSTRIAL METALS SPLIT: traders LONG China-reflation (Cu +2.5%, Ag +2.6%), SHORT war-premium-supply (Al -2.4%) = structural disagreement with energy market still paying crisis premium.** ORIGINAL CONTEXT (May 12): **US DECOUPLING ESSENTIALLY HOLDING DESPITE WAR ESCALATION: S&P 500 7,412.84 fresh record close +0.2% 1D / +8.7% 1M / +8.5% 3M; NASDAQ 26,274.13 +0.1% 1D / +14.7% 1M / +16.3% 3M; Dow 49,704.47 +0.2% 1D. SMH $576.31 +1.7% 1D / +31.9% 1M / +41.9% 3M (AI-CapEx demand exceeding helium-constraint impact). VIX +6.9% 1D to 18.38 — first warning US complacency cracking but still NOT panic (cycle low ~13-14 late April; March crisis-peak 32+).** US 10Y yield 4.41% (+1.1% 1D, +2.2% 1M, +5.7% 3M) — oil-driven inflation expectations keeping real yields competitive vs zero-coupon gold; 2Y 3.60%; DXY 98.13 (+0.2% 1D). **Drivers of VIX +6.9%:** Trump 'TOTALLY UNACCEPTABLE' rejection + WH Sit-Room meeting May 11 weighing renewed strikes + Hormuz transit deteriorated to 2-5 ships/day per Aramco + Agios Fanourios VLCC U-turn + Houthi-attribution-unconfirmed strike near Hodeidah possibly breaking Day 80 quiet. **Sectoral repricing today:** XLE +2.6% 1D (Brent up + Exxon +3.5% — sector breadth strongest in 5 sessions); CF Industries +8.2% 1D (THE MOVE OF THE DAY — Modi's fertilizer-50% appeal + UBS PT $97→$140); Nutrien +4.3%; BASF +3.5% (fertilizer chain); Linde +2.3% (industrial-gas pricing power); Uranium (URA) +3.7% 1D / +12.3% 1M (Iran HEU concession + return clause = nuclear file now central diplomatic axis); Exxon +3.5% 1D (Brent + Lavan offline); Lockheed +1.1% 1D / -16.5% 1M (defense-rotation paradox: Q1 miss April 23 EPS $6.44 vs $7.28; FCF -$291M; supply-chain delays + fixed-cost-program margin compression; ITA -1.6% 1M); Frontline -3.4% 1D / +11.1% 1M (Evercore ISI downgrade citing 2027-28 oversupply); Cheniere $240.70 +0.5% 1D / -9.1% 1M. **Wheat +3.2%, Corn +3.2% (fertilizer cost pass-through); RBOB Gasoline -3.7% 1D (counter-trend mean-reversion after +80% 3M).** **US-vs-India decoupling is STRUCTURAL story of the day:** US +0.2% record vs Indian Sensex -1.7% May 11. Aluminum LME -2.3% 1D = COUNTER-NARRATIVE (cancelled-warrant decline -7% to 26,500t signals reduced near-term physical demand; China property + global industrial moderation; not all crisis-linked moves are crisis-driven). **Watch level: VIX >20 confirms regime shift.** Until then, rotation signal not regime change.

active_triggers.condition: VIX spikes 17→28+ within 48hr of Iran MOU response (~by 2026-05-09) — peace-dividend reversal test
S&P 500 enters bear market (down 20% from peak)
VIX cash breaks above 22 on Wednesday ceasefire lapse (binary escalation-hedging threshold)
VIX gaps above 24 within 14 days (by 2026-05-12) on any escalation event = mispricing reverses; equity-vol catches up to oil-vol
VIX trend-break confirmation — VIX prints 22+ within 5 sessions sustained (regime shift signal)

recent_signals.headline: **S&P 500 FLAT TO DOWN MARGINALLY DESPITE TRUMP POSTPONEMENT — RATES OVERRIDING GEOPOLITICS.** S&P 7,403.05 (-0.1% 1D), NASDAQ 26,090.73 (-0.5% 1D), Dow 49,686.12 (+0.3% 1D). **The flat-to-down close despite a postponement-relief setup is the cleanest signal that US 30Y at 5.13% (10-month high) is now the dominant macro driver, not Iran tail risk.** Bloomberg framed last week as 'worst weekly Treasury rout in a year.' Postponement of the Iran strike removed a tail risk but did not remove the structural rate pressure from PPI +1.4% m/m, Warsh effective May 16, oil pass-through, and inflation-persistence framing from Fed officials. **VIX -3.3% to 17.82 confirms the tail-risk de-pricing**; the equity-market tape confirms the underlying macro is unchanged. **For the investor: the postponement is bearish for short-vol positioning that gets stopped in a market that doesn't snap back; defense-sector idiosyncratic catalysts (Lockheed $879M F-35) drove ITA +1.4% on a market-flat day; energy XLE +1.9% on Brent-down day is the structural-bid signal.**
sources: Intel dossier 2026-05-19 A2; Markets dossier D1, C3, C4
recent_signals.verification: S&P -0.1% / NASDAQ -0.5% / Dow +0.3% = CONFIRMED operational tape; US 30Y 5.13% 10-month high = CONFIRMED; Bloomberg framing = CONFIRMED.

recent_signals.headline: **C3 CRISIS-TRADE UNWIND CONTINUES — S&P 500 7,444.25 (+0.6% 1D / +6.8% 1M / +8.8% 3M); NASDAQ +1.2% / +11.7% 1M; Dow -0.1%; VIX 17.87 SUB-18 on a 1-week-clock-day.** Markets pricing Trump-Xi pause. **Lockheed Martin $519.94 (-0.2% 1D, -15.0% 1M, -19.5% 3M) = DEFENSE DE-RATING IN MOTION** — single largest move in bellwether complex signalling market is unambiguously pricing crisis maturation, not escalation; ITA -4.1% 1M confirms broader rotation. **CF Industries -3.8% 1D** = Modi fertilizer-cut-50% appeal + Gulf urea normalization (~80% crisis-unwind). **Cheniere -2.0% 1D / -7.6% 1M** = Q1 $3.5bn hedge loss + LNG infrastructure unwind. **Frontline -3.5% 1D** = Evercore downgrade despite VLCC TD3C ~$420K/day spike — paper-physical bifurcation within same company. **Aluminum -5.6% 1D + RBOB -4.5% 1D = position unwind + summit-signal**. Agriculture rally on Black Sea + Trump-Xi tariff dialogue: Wheat +2.7% / +15% 1M; Corn +3.2%; Rough Rice +2.6%; Sugar +2.5% (Brazil + BRL +1.5%). **MSCI EM ETF (EEM) +2.1% 1D = clean Trump-Xi market vote.** Bovespa -1.8% / -10.3% 1M = idiosyncratic (Bolsonaro-Banco Master scandal + Brazil inflation). US 10Y 4.48% (CPI absorption, Fed easing closed). **A2 1-week clock closed today without strike order** — forced-pause-via-Beijing validated. Trump HEU-via-China door; Vance softer; Pentagon Sledgehammer. **Per markets dossier H: VIX 12-14 if MOU signs by May 21; VIX 26-32 if Hormuz remains closed through June; if summit fails to deliver framework, VIX +30% spike to 23-24 by Monday is base case.**

recent_signals.headline: A2 APRIL CPI 3.8% ABSORBED — S&P 500 -0.29% May 12 to 7,392 (off May 11 record 7,412.84); NASDAQ -0.7% to 26,088.20; Dow +0.1% to 49,760.56. SMH -2.6% (profit-take after vertical AI run + TAIEX -1.3% sympathy + rotation from AI-mega-caps to industrial-cyclicals — Cu +2.5%, Ag +2.6%). **C1 VIX FELL -2.1% TO 17.99 = THE PARADOX OF THE DAY** — expected on normal escalation+CPI+Litani day: VIX +5-10%; got -2.1%. **Markets pricing TRUMP-IN-BEIJING as forced kinetic pause through May 15-16 = 48-hour vol kill via timeline mechanics**; EIA forecast cut to $94.85 (TASS) institutional vote against Brent fear premium; Aramco+ADNOC quiet limited Hormuz resume confirms physical supply ~90% preserved through East-West routing; Iran missile fire rate -92% (JPost+CNN intel) = capability-vs-willingness gap publicly quantified. Investor asymmetric: if Trump returns from Beijing without order → VIX 16-handle + Brent $97-100; if order → VIX +30% intraday + Brent $115-120 (BINARY DIGITAL).
